subject: [Consul] Fixing bug in consul
commit_message:
[Consul] Fixing bug in consul

Fixing issue with zombie processes generated by consul

Issue-ID: OOM-2630
Signed-off-by: Daniel Milaszkiewicz <daniel.milaszkiewicz@nokia.com>
Change-Id: I0c10450b1ec0872862bb5f3a2ff0a1a1f0840bef

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

BTW
I've not observed this issue thus more details would be rally helpful additionally fix in this form cannot be accepted as it brings back the root process

Patch Set 1:

Krzysztof, your previous commit introduce some kind of DoS attack into ONAP. Consule generates a lot of defunct processes and kills whole worker because OS cannot open any new processes. After a while consules starts on other worker and situation starts again and next worker were killed. Please check what is corrected in your commit and merge it asap.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Krzysztof, your previous commit introduce some kind of DoS attack into ONAP. Consule generates a lot of defunct processes and kills whole worker because OS cannot open any new processes. After a while consules starts on other worker and situation starts again and next worker were killed. Please check what is corrected in your commit and merge it asap.

Daniel,
I'm not negating that I've introduced a bug. All what I say is that:
a) I've not observed this thus I'd be more than happy to get more details. ie list of zombie processes (when I run it locally I've none). For an issue like this I'd expect a very precise and descriptive error message. If I could get more details on where are the zombie processes coming from maybe we could find a better solution than reintroducing root process

b) It's not the proper way of fixing this issue as it reintroduces a bug that we've already fixed.

We can either workout a proper fix for this or instead of silently reintroducing the issue just send a revert for my commit which would be more proper than this patch.

Patch Set 1:

In correction there is no reverting to previous solution - it is corrected only your command. You forget to add "-c" do sh command. This is still your solution but corrected. Still your commmit - nothing changed.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> In correction there is no reverting to previous solution - it is corrected only your command. You forget to add "-c" do sh command. This is still your solution but corrected. Still your commmit - nothing changed.

Nothing has been forgotten;) It was intentional.

There is a major difference between
sh -c /path/to/script
and 
sh /path/to/script

in first case you just spawn a shell and run a script as a command, in second case you start an interpreter that starts interpreting the script as it is and ignoring #!/my/interp which in this case causes the dumb-init to run as a root process
Removed Code-Review+1 by Morgan Richomme <morgan.richomme@orange.com>

Patch Set 1:

Hi Krzysztof, I've attached to the bug report 2 logs with outputs of ps showing zombie/defunct processes in consul pod (one containing output from pod, 2nd from worker).
Here some small part of it:
ubuntu@onap-6957-rke-node:~$ kubectl -n onap exec -it dev-consul-5f489df7c5-tlxzp -- ps -o pid,ppid,user,etime,stat,args | head -20
PID   PPID  USER     ELAPSED STAT COMMAND
    1     0 consul    56:43  S    consul agent -data-dir=/consul/data -config-d
  360     1 consul    44:36  Z    [busybox]
  361     1 consul    44:36  Z    [curl]
  377     1 consul    32:50  Z    [busybox]
  378     1 consul    32:50  Z    [curl]
  391     1 consul    10:16  Z    [busybox]
  392     1 consul    10:16  Z    [curl]
  407     1 consul    21:23  Z    [busybox]
  408     1 consul    21:23  Z    [curl]
  427     1 consul    21:22  Z    [busybox]
  428     1 consul    21:22  Z    [curl]
  510     1 consul    44:34  Z    [busybox]
  512     1 consul    44:34  Z    [curl]
  525     1 consul    44:33  Z    [busybox]
  526     1 consul    44:33  Z    [curl]
  531     1 consul    10:12  Z    [busybox]
  532     1 consul    10:12  Z    [curl]
  535     1 consul    10:11  Z    [busybox]
  536     1 consul    10:11  Z    [curl]


Btw. As far as I know, Bartek also observed such behaviour.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Btw. As far as I know, Bartek also observed such behaviour.

Affirmative. Things on k8s host in my deployments looked exactly like Krzysztof Kosewski described. In the end all k8s host rendered unusable:

[root@infra ~]# kubectl get nodes
NAME    STATUS     ROLES               AGE     VERSION
infra   Ready      controlplane,etcd   6d12h   v1.15.9
node0   NotReady   worker              6d12h   v1.15.9
node1   NotReady   worker              6d12h   v1.15.9
node2   NotReady   worker              6d12h   v1.15.9

It starts with system reaching file-max limit (too much forked processes), then docker engine crashes and in the end k8s host can't be even pinged.
